pages.full_text:
off, so we cycled back to the van. Tea, yoga, showers. A super day. Glorious hot [?Lunebike].
The French seem to speak very odd French here.
Each day I have attempted a sudoku & started a crossword & Simon has finished the crossword.
None of the loos or showers have any hooks.

Saturday
We left at 8.40. for Calais. lovely sunny day.
We drove to the Channel Tunnel & followed a handsome Ferrari onto the train.
In England we visited Peter. Nena & Joconda have a bad chest. Conrad had spent 2 hours playing football in 30° & gave me a very sweaty hug when I arrived. Peter had been watching Nini's ballet performance – a "bizarre experience." I took Nini & Joconda to the
pages.notes: Page begins mid-sentence, continuing from previous page. '[?Lunebike]' is unclear — could be a campsite name, a place, or possibly a reference to the cycling route (Véloroute Vallée de la Somme shown on the glued-in map). A printed/glued-in map of northern France is present on the left side of the page, showing a cycling route around the Somme estuary area; places visible on the map include Tadorne (or Namport-Saint-N[?]), Heron, Gravelot, Le Crotoy, Saint-Valery-sur-Somme, Noyelles sur Mer, Abbeville, Veloroute Val[lée de la Somme]. Annotations next to map: 'Noyelles sur Mer' and 'Abbeville'. Text continues onto right page ('I took Nini & Joconda to the'). 'Joconda' is the diary's spelling (may be 'Gioconda' or similar). No handwritten page number visible in top-left corner.

pages.full_text: Shops & the park. Then after chatting & watching a bit of cricket we left for home.
pages.notes: Handwritten page number 34 visible in top-right corner. Only two lines of text at the top of the page; the rest is blank. This is a continuation from the left page ('I took Nini & Joconda to the / Shops & the park...'). The left margin shows gutter overflow text from the left page (Channel Tunnel, 'the', 'Peter.', etc.) — not transcribed again here.
